stories.headline: DeVonta Smith Inherits the Whole Saloon in Philly
stories.read: Well pardner, with A.J. Brown shipped off to New England, DeVonta Smith is the undisputed top gun in Philadelphia for the first time since his rookie days. When Brown sat last year, Smith's cut of the throws jumped from under 21 percent to 29 percent, and he posted 75-plus yards in every game without him. The saloon's still pricin' him as a back-end WR1 around No. 14, but all the air yards and red-zone looks just landed in his lap. Go stake that claim before the herd catches on.

stories.headline: Bhayshul Tuten: The Vein Nobody's Struck Yet in Jacksonville
stories.read: Hold up and listen close, 'cause this one's cheap gold. Travis Etienne rode off to New Orleans, and that leaves second-year man Bhayshul Tuten staked to Jacksonville's lead-back job, with analysts pegging him for 60-65 percent of the reins in that backfield. He was the fastest critter in his draft class and ranked top-five in rushing success as a rookie, and now he runs in Liam Coen's offense that's minted breakout backs before. He's goin' off the board as a mid-round RB3 with bell-cow upside — that's three rounds of value past where the saloon grabs him. Pan that creek now.

stories.headline: Achane Laid Up at Minicamp — But Don't Spook the Herd
stories.read: Doc's got a soft flag, not a hard one, pardner. De'Von Achane's been kept to individual drills in Miami's minicamp, nursin' a shoulder after an offseason clean-up procedure. Before you go gettin' jumpy, every sign says he's good to ride for training camp in July — and the Dolphins just backed up the wagon with a four-year, $68 million extension. After 1,838 scrimmage yards and 12 scores on 305 touches last year, this is a buy-the-dip moment, not a dry well. Let your leaguemates flinch while you keep him saddled.
